name: firewall_change
description: a firewall rule-change management world; actions request, review, apply, rollback

card.description: a firewall rule-change management world; actions request, review, apply, rollback

rules: A change is identified by an incrementing request_id and carries a rule text, a requester, and a declared risk level.
'request' opens a new change only when none is in flight (status is idle, applied, rejected, or rolled_back); otherwise it is a no-op.
'review' acts only on a 'requested' change: decision 'approve' -> approved, any other decision -> rejected (pending rule cleared).
'apply' takes effect only on an 'approved' change, adding its rule to active_rules and marking it the last_applied_rule for rollback.
'rollback' acts only on an 'applied' change, removing last_applied_rule from active_rules.
Separation of duties: no rule reaches 'applied' without passing through 'requested' then 'approved' review.
Every state change appends one line to the append-only audit_log.

transition.code:
def transition(state, action):
    s = dict(state)
    name = action.get("name")
    params = action.get("params") or {}
    actor = action.get("agent") or params.get("actor") or "unknown"
    s["active_rules"] = list(s.get("active_rules", []))
    s["audit_log"] = list(s.get("audit_log", []))

    def log(event):
        s["audit_log"] = s["audit_log"] + [event]

    status = s.get("status", "idle")

    if name == "request":
        # A new request can only begin when no change is in flight.
        if status in ("idle", "applied", "rejected", "rolled_back"):
            rid = int(s.get("request_id", 0)) + 1
            rule = params.get("rule") or s.get("pending_rule") or (
                "allow tcp 0.0.0.0/0 -> 10.0.0.%d:443" % rid)
            s["request_id"] = rid
            s["status"] = "requested"
            s["pending_rule"] = rule
            s["requested_by"] = params.get("requested_by") or actor
            s["risk"] = params.get("risk") or s.get("risk") or "unassessed"
            s["reviewed_by"] = ""
            s["review_decision"] = ""
            s["requested_count"] = int(s.get("requested_count", 0)) + 1
            log("request#%d by %s: %s" % (rid, s["requested_by"], rule))
        # else: a change is already requested/approved -> ignore (no-op)

    elif name == "review":
        # Only a requested (not yet reviewed) change can be reviewed.
        if status == "requested":
            decision = params.get("decision") or "approve"
            reviewer = params.get("reviewer") or actor
            s["reviewed_by"] = reviewer
            s["review_decision"] = decision
            if decision == "approve":
                s["status"] = "approved"
                log("review#%d by %s: APPROVED" % (s.get("request_id", 0), reviewer))
            else:
                s["status"] = "rejected"
                s["rejected_count"] = int(s.get("rejected_count", 0)) + 1
                log("review#%d by %s: REJECTED" % (s.get("request_id", 0), reviewer))
                s["pending_rule"] = ""

    elif name == "apply":
        # Only an approved change reaches the live firewall.
        if status == "approved":
            rule = s.get("pending_rule", "")
            if rule and rule not in s["active_rules"]:
                s["active_rules"] = s["active_rules"] + [rule]
            s["last_applied_rule"] = rule
            s["status"] = "applied"
            s["applied_count"] = int(s.get("applied_count", 0)) + 1
            log("apply#%d by %s: %s now LIVE" % (s.get("request_id", 0), actor, rule))

    elif name == "rollback":
        # Only an applied change can be undone, removing its rule from the firewall.
        if status == "applied":
            rule = s.get("last_applied_rule", "")
            s["active_rules"] = [r for r in s["active_rules"] if r != rule]
            s["status"] = "rolled_back"
            s["rollback_count"] = int(s.get("rollback_count", 0)) + 1
            log("rollback#%d by %s: %s removed" % (s.get("request_id", 0), actor, rule))

    return s

emit.report: Firewall change [{status}] pending={pending_rule!r} risk={risk} | {applied_count} applied, {rollback_count} rolled back | live rules: {active_rules}

objectives.name: controlled_changes
objectives.goal: maximize the share of applied rules that were reviewed and approved

objectives.name: minimize_rollbacks
objectives.goal: minimize rollback_count (fewer bad changes reaching production)

objectives.name: throughput
objectives.goal: maximize applied_count of legitimately approved changes
